name: Chickens
description: Versatile poultry raised for both eggs and meat. Dual-purpose breeds like Rhode Island Red or Plymouth Rock provide steady egg production and reasonable meat yield. Broilers reach market weight in 6-8 weeks.
feed: Commercial layer/broiler feed (16-20% protein), supplemented with grit, calcium (oyster shell for layers), kitchen scraps, and forage. Approximately 120g of feed per bird per day for layers, 150g for broilers.
care_instructions: Provide secure coop with ventilation and predator protection. Clean bedding (straw or wood shavings) weekly. Fresh water daily. Collect eggs daily. Dust bath area recommended. Vaccinate for Marek's disease. Deworm quarterly. Provide 14-16 hours of light for consistent laying.
yield_frequency: Eggs: daily (250-300/year for layers). Meat: one-time harvest at 8-12 weeks for broilers, 16-20 weeks for dual-purpose.

name: Rabbits
description: Efficient small livestock producing lean, high-protein meat with excellent feed conversion ratio. Breeds like New Zealand White and Californian reach market weight of 2-2.5 kg dressed in 8-12 weeks. Does can produce 4-5 litters per year.
feed: Commercial rabbit pellets (16-18% protein), unlimited timothy or grass hay, fresh leafy greens daily. Approximately 150-200g pellets per adult per day plus unlimited hay. Fresh water via bottle or bowl.
care_instructions: House in wire-bottom hutches or colony pens with solid resting areas. Clean droppings trays 2-3 times weekly. Protect from extreme heat (above 85°F is dangerous). Provide shade and ventilation. Check teeth and nails monthly. Breed does at 5-6 months. Separate bucks. Nest box 3 days before kindling.
yield_frequency: Meat: harvest at 8-12 weeks (fryers). A single doe can produce 4-5 litters of 6-10 kits per year, yielding 25-50 fryers annually.

name: Pigs
description: Fast-growing livestock reaching market weight of 110-120 kg in 5-6 months. Excellent foragers that convert kitchen scraps and garden waste into high-quality pork. Heritage breeds like Berkshire and Tamworth offer superior flavor.
feed: Commercial grower feed (14-18% protein), supplemented with garden produce, whey, kitchen scraps, and pasture forage. Finishing pigs consume 3-4 kg of feed per day. Feed conversion ratio approximately 3.5:1.
care_instructions: Provide sturdy fencing (electric or hog panels). Shelter from sun and rain. Mud wallow or sprinkler in summer (pigs cannot sweat). Fresh water 10-15 liters per day. Rotate pasture to prevent soil damage. Deworm regularly. Avoid feeding raw meat or garbage. Socialize early for easier handling.
yield_frequency: Meat: one-time harvest at 5-6 months (market weight ~110 kg live, ~70 kg carcass yield at 60-65% dressing). Sows can farrow 2 litters per year of 10-14 piglets each.

name: Cows
description: Large ruminants raised for beef, dairy, or both. Beef breeds like Angus and Hereford finish at 500-600 kg in 18-24 months. Dairy breeds like Jersey and Holstein produce 20-30 liters of milk daily. Require significant land and infrastructure.
feed: Pasture grass (primary), hay in winter, grain supplement for finishing or high-production dairy. Beef cattle consume 2-3% of body weight in dry matter daily. Dairy cows need higher energy rations with grain, silage, and mineral supplements.
care_instructions: Provide adequate pasture with rotational grazing. Shelter from extreme weather. Fresh water 40-80 liters per day. Annual veterinary checkups and vaccinations. Hoof trimming 2-3 times yearly for dairy. Mineral salt licks. Calving assistance may be needed. Fencing must be strong (barbed wire or high-tensile).
yield_frequency: Meat: one-time harvest at 18-24 months (carcass ~250 kg from 550 kg live weight at ~60% dressing). Dairy: 305-day lactation cycle producing 5,000-10,000 liters per year depending on breed.

name: Quails
description: Compact poultry ideal for small spaces. Coturnix quail mature in just 6-8 weeks and begin laying at 6 weeks of age. Produce rich, speckled eggs and delicate meat. Can often be kept where chickens are prohibited.
feed: Game bird feed (24-28% protein for chicks, 20-24% for adults). Approximately 25-35g per bird per day. Provide grit and calcium supplement for layers. Fresh water via nipple drinkers or shallow dishes.
care_instructions: House in wire cages or ground pens with 1 sq ft per bird minimum. Low ceiling height (12-18 inches) prevents flush injuries. Collect eggs daily. Clean droppings frequently due to high moisture content. Protect from drafts and predators. Keep in groups with 1 male per 3-5 females. Short lifespan (2-3 years productive).
yield_frequency: Eggs: nearly daily (280-320 eggs/year per hen). Meat: harvest at 6-8 weeks. Quick generation time allows continuous production year-round.

name: Ducks
description: Hardy waterfowl raised for meat, eggs, and pest control. Pekin ducks reach market weight in 7-8 weeks. Khaki Campbells lay 250-340 eggs per year. Excellent foragers that consume slugs, snails, and insects.
feed: Waterfowl or all-flock feed (16-20% protein). Approximately 170-200g per bird per day. Supplement with forage, greens, and insects. Niacin supplement important for ducklings (brewer's yeast). Fresh water deep enough to submerge head.
care_instructions: Provide predator-proof shelter with good ventilation. No roosts needed (ducks sleep on ground). Access to water for head dipping minimum, swimming optional but beneficial. Clean bedding frequently (ducks are messy with water). No need for nesting boxes—ducks lay on ground. Hardy in cold weather. Protect from extreme heat.
yield_frequency: Eggs: daily for prolific breeds (250-340/year for Khaki Campbell, 150-200 for Pekin). Meat: harvest at 7-9 weeks for Pekin (2.5 kg dressed weight from 3.5 kg live).

name: Sheep
description: Versatile ruminants raised for meat (lamb/mutton), wool, and milk. Meat breeds like Suffolk and Dorper reach market weight of 45-55 kg in 5-7 months. Wool breeds like Merino produce 4-10 kg of fleece annually. Excellent grazers for land management.
feed: Pasture grass (primary), hay in winter. Grain supplement for finishing lambs and lactating ewes. Consume 2-4% of body weight in dry matter daily. Mineral supplement with selenium and copper (species-specific—copper toxic at high levels). Fresh water 4-8 liters per day.
care_instructions: Provide fencing (woven wire or electric net). Shelter from rain and wind. Shear wool breeds annually in spring. Trim hooves every 6-8 weeks. Deworm based on fecal egg counts. Vaccinate for clostridial diseases. Watch for fly strike in warm weather. Lambing assistance may be needed. Guard animals recommended for predator protection.
yield_frequency: Meat: harvest lambs at 5-8 months (carcass ~20 kg from 45 kg live at ~44-50% dressing). Wool: shear once annually (4-10 kg fleece). Milk: 1-3 liters/day during 150-240 day lactation. Ewes typically lamb once per year (1-3 lambs).

name: Goats
description: Agile browsers raised for meat, milk, and fiber. Meat breeds like Boer reach 35-50 kg market weight in 4-6 months. Dairy breeds like Nigerian Dwarf and Nubian produce 1-4 liters of rich milk daily. Excellent brush clearers.
feed: Browse and pasture (primary), grass hay, alfalfa for dairy and growing kids. Grain supplement for lactating does and growing kids. Consume 3-5% of body weight in dry matter daily. Loose mineral supplement (copper important for goats). Fresh water 4-8 liters per day.
care_instructions: Provide strong fencing (goats are escape artists—use 4-5 ft woven wire or cattle panels). Shelter from rain (goats dislike getting wet). Trim hooves every 4-6 weeks. Deworm based on FAMACHA scoring. Vaccinate for CDT (clostridial diseases and tetanus). Disbud kids at 3-7 days if desired. Keep in pairs minimum (herd animals). Watch for bloat and parasites.
yield_frequency: Meat: harvest kids at 4-8 months (carcass ~15 kg from 35 kg live at ~40-50% dressing). Milk: 1-4 liters/day during 10-month lactation. Fiber: shear Angora/Cashmere breeds 1-2 times yearly. Does typically kid once per year (1-3 kids).
